title: Gujarat CM calls for advance planning in water conservation ahead of monsoon - The News Mill
article_text:
Gujarat Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel has urged district collectors to undertake advance preparations for water conservation well before the forthcoming monsoon to maximise rainwater harvesting and storage, thereby maintaining Gujarat’s leading status in this area, the Chief Minister’s Office stated in a press release.

Discover more Job Listings Platform Politics Local food delivery

The Chief Minister made this appeal during a video conference jointly organised by the Ministry of Jal Shakti, Government of India, and the State Government to conduct a comprehensive review of the implementation of the Jal Sanchay Jan Bhagidari 2.0 campaign in Gujarat.

Union Minister for Jal Shakti C R Patil and Department Secretary Kantha Rao, together with other central government officials, participated from Delhi, while Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel, Chief Secretary M K Das, and senior secretaries joined from Gandhinagar.

District collectors representing various districts, including Banaskantha, Kutch, and Rajkot, presented detailed updates on the progress of the Jal Sanchay Jan Bhagidari 2.0 campaign in their areas and outlined future plans.

The Chief Minister stated, “The visionary leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi provides clear guidance to address future challenges and find effective solutions.” He added that the focus remains on ensuring Gujarat benefits maximally from the nationwide Catch the Rain campaign and the Prime Minister’s national water conservation movement.

He urged district collectors to plan rainwater harvesting and storage works with a long-term perspective, remarking, “These public welfare efforts bring both a sense of duty fulfilled and personal satisfaction.” Further, he advocated fostering an environment of healthy competition among districts implementing the Jal Sanchay Jan Bhagidari campaign, encouraging those with fewer activities to increase their efforts.

Chief Minister Patel highlighted that the state government has allocated a grant of Rs 50 lakh to each MLA for water conservation and storage works, emphasising that these funds must be utilised for water conservation projects in the districts.

He also urged prompt implementation of extensive water conservation measures to ensure full utilisation of the Rs 553 crore grant provided to Gujarat by the central government under the Jal Sanchay Jan Bhagidari scheme by March 2026.

Union Minister for Jal Shakti C R Patil praised Gujarat’s water conservation and storage efforts as a national model. He noted, “The decision for the central government to bear 90 per cent of the cost for recharging old borewells has resulted in significant benefits,” and stressed the importance of preparing a list of NGOs to motivate them to participate more actively in water conservation activities.

He added that under the Viksit Bharat – Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) (VB-G RAM G), implemented under the Prime Minister’s guidance, 40 per cent of scheme funds are allocated for water conservation and storage work.

Providing guidance on the timely and planned utilisation of funds under this scheme, he stated that, “By constructing water structures using scientific methods and progressively enhancing water conservation and storage capacity, the Prime Minister’s resolve of water security and water self-reliance can be achieved.”

He outlined the objective of constructing over ten crore water harvesting structures nationwide by May 31, 2026, as part of the Jal Sanchay Jan Bhagidari 2.0 campaign, and suggested that Gujarat complete the majority of its water conservation works before the upcoming monsoon.

Under the initial Jal Sanchay Jan Bhagidari 1 campaign, Gujarat completed a total of 1,33,522 water conservation works. The meeting also reviewed the significant benefits realised in groundwater recharge through methods such as rainwater harvesting, recharge tubewells, pond deepening, farm ponds, filter wells, and similar initiatives.

Chief Secretary M K Das advised all district collectors to operate in mission mode to ensure the successful implementation of the Jal Sanchay Jan Bhagidari 2.0 campaign.

The review meeting was attended by Minister of State for Water Supply Ishwarsinh Patel, Principal Secretary Dhananjay Dwivedi, Additional Principal Secretary to the Chief Minister Dr Vikrant Pandey, Secretary Dr Ajay Kumar, and Water Resources Secretary P C Vyas.
